name: Zhang Fei (Yide)

novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	The Peach Tree Garden oath did not actually take place but Sanguozhi states that Liu, Guan and Zhang ‘slept in the same bed and were as close as brothers.’
	Zhang Fei did not whip the court official when drunk in Anxi. It was Liu Bei, and he was not drunk. [SGZ: Liu Bei]
	Zhang Fei did not kill Cao Bao for drinking, they had a ‘falling out’. Cao Bao was not special to Lü Bu, but his death caused riots in the city. Yuan Shu corresponded with Lü Bu and it was Xu Dan that opened the gates. [ZZTJ: 62]
	Never shouted at Xiahou Jie, causing his death.
	Historically, Zhang Fei was a skilled military leader and more intelligent than the novel portrays.

	 Simplified Chinese

	 Pronunciation

	 Pinyin (Tones)

	 Wade-Giles

	 Birthplace 
	Zhuo commandery

	 Name Notes 
	Sanguozhi records Zhang Fei’s style as 益 (yi4: ‘benefit’ or ‘increase’) rather than 翼 (yi4: ‘wing’), as used in the novel, games, and most literature.

full_text:
Zhang Fei (Yide) 張飛 (益德)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Zhang Fei (張飛), styledYide (益德), was born in Zhuo commandery. A loyal companion of Liu Bei and a ferocious general. Brutal to his men. Zhang Fei was affiliated with the Han Dynasty and the Shu Kingdom. Zhang Fei lived during the Later Han Dynasty and Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.
Return to theThree Kingdoms Encyclopediaor browse theEncyclopedia Directory.

Fact vs Fiction
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
The Peach Tree Garden oath did not actually take place butSanguozhistates that Liu, Guan and Zhang ‘slept in the same bed and wereas close as brothers.’
Zhang Fei did not whip the court official when drunk in Anxi. It was Liu Bei, and he was not drunk. [SGZ: Liu Bei]
Zhang Fei did not kill Cao Bao for drinking, they had a ‘falling out’. Cao Bao was not special to Lü Bu, but his death caused riots in the city. Yuan Shu corresponded with Lü Bu and it was Xu Dan that opened the gates. [ZZTJ: 62]
Never shouted at Xiahou Jie, causing his death.
Historically, Zhang Fei was a skilled military leader and more intelligent than the novel portrays.
Literary Appearances
Romance of the Three Kingdoms:1–2,5–7,11–22,24–25,28,31,34–43,45,49–53,55–57,60–61,63–65,69–73,76–78,80,81,83,85,120
Sanguozhi:Shu 6, Shu 11

[“三姓家奴休走！燕人張飛在此！”]
